title: Phone and Email Basics
description: The fixed phrases that make phone calls and emails feel effortless. Opening, holding, transferring, signing off.
summary_pt_br: Fórmulas fixas para telefone e e-mail em inglês. Telefone: Hello, this is X. May I speak to...? I'll put you through. E-mail: Dear / Hi, I'm writing to... / Looking forward to hearing from you / Best regards.

scenes.text_en:
Joca's phone rang. 'HELLO, THIS IS JOCA.'
'HI Joca, it's Sarah from Stripe. MAY I SPEAK TO the manager?'
'SURE, HOLD ON. I'll PUT YOU THROUGH.'
Later, Joca opened his email: 'DEAR Sarah, I'M WRITING TO confirm…'
'LOOKING FORWARD TO your reply. BEST REGARDS, Joca.'
scenes.text_pt:
O celular do Joca tocou. 'OI, AQUI É O JOCA.'
'Oi Joca, aqui é a Sarah do Stripe. POSSO FALAR COM o gestor?'
'CLARO, UM MOMENTO. Vou TRANSFERIR.'
Depois, Joca abriu o e-mail: 'PREZADA Sarah, ESTOU ESCREVENDO para confirmar…'
'NA EXPECTATIVA da sua resposta. ATENCIOSAMENTE, Joca.'

items.term: I hope this email finds you well
Looking forward to hearing from you

scenes.title: Phone-English identities
scenes.body_en: In Portuguese you say 'É o Joca'. In English NEVER 'I am Joca' on the phone. Use 'THIS IS JOCA.' To ask the person: 'WHO'S CALLING?' not 'who are you?'. Every single phone call uses these fixed forms — memorize them as blocks.
scenes.body_pt: Em português você diz 'É o Joca'. Em inglês NUNCA 'I am Joca' no telefone. Use 'THIS IS JOCA'. Para perguntar quem: 'WHO'S CALLING?' não 'who are you?'. Decore como blocos.

examples.en: Hello, this is Bia — who's calling?
examples.pt: Alô, aqui é a Bia — quem está falando?

exercise.question: You answer the phone. Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: THIS IS [NAME] — the fixed phone identity phrase.

exercise.question: Complete an email: 'I'm writing ___ confirm our meeting.'
